title: Creating webhook for sub-items returning null (and no challange)

posts.author: daniel1 (Daniel Rudaev)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi!
I’m trying to create a new webhook for a sub-items board.
I got the board id from quarying the item, and then I mounted it into that -
mutation {
create_webhook ( board_id: 633286485, url: “https://webhook.site/d2e3ydh0-943a-47ea-ade1-ba84fa98f61b”, event: change_column_value) {
id
board_id
}
}

The issue is that the response returns null -
{
“data”: {
“create_webhook”: null
},
“account_id”: 9729487
}

I tried it with “normal” boards, and it worked well… So maybe there is kind of blocking engaging sub-items “ghost” boards?

posts.author: dsilva (Daniel Silva)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @daniel1 - welcome!!

Could you expand a bit more on what you’re referring to by “sub-item ghost boards” versus normal boards? If you have any screenshots you wouldn’t mind posting, that would be a big help.

-Daniel

posts.author: daniel1 (Daniel Rudaev)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you for your comment @dsilva
I wish to create a webhook that will trigger every time there is a status change in my sub-items. As I understood, the sub-items are saved in a type of “ghost board” that is not visible thru the Monday interface but can be engaged thru API. So I got that board id from querying a sub-item.
When trying to create that webhook, using that board id, it returns null and does not send any “challenge” key. When trying to make normal webhooks (for regular boards), it works fine.

image1604×604 28.6 KB

I hope I was able to explain the matter better.
Many thanks,
Daniel

posts.author: dsilva (Daniel Silva)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @daniel1 - great catch! Thank you for providing that extra information, it’s a huge help.

I was able to reproduce this issue with the information you provided above. I’ve gone ahead and documented this with our team so that we can take care of this on our end, however I wouldn’t quite be able to give you an ETA on this at the moment.

Please let us know if we can help with anything else.

-Daniel

posts.author: daniel1 (Daniel Rudaev)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @dsilva!
Is there any news with that?

posts.author: dsilva (Daniel Silva)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @daniel1 - not just yet. This is still something we’re looking at internally as we find the best way to tackle this, unfortunately I don’t have an ETA just yet.

-Daniel

posts.author: daniel1 (Daniel Rudaev)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @dsilva!
Do you have an ETA for this feature?

posts.author: Helen (Helen)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @daniel1! Stepping in here.

Sadly, we don’t yet have an ETA for this.

Rest assured that once we’ve implemented this though, we will be sure to make a community announcement. Thank you for your patience!

posts.author: AlexSavchuk (Alex Savchuk)
posts.cookedHtml:
@LucasG

As mentioned earlier in a different thread, I’m afraid that we don’t have any updates just yet. I do recommend sticking to posting in one topic at a time, if they both seem to discuss the same feature. As the other topic has more votes (10, compared to 0 here), I’m going to close this topic to avoid further confusion. I hope that makes sense!

-Alex
